close_reason: Already fixed: inventory closures use imported Arc
description:
src/lib.rs lines 244-259 use `std::sync::Arc::new(b) as std::sync::Arc<dyn ...>` in both inventory::submit! blocks. Sibling crate secretx-aws-sm imports `use std::sync::Arc;` at the top of the file and uses bare `Arc::new` / `Arc<dyn ...>` in the same place.

Either form compiles. The aws-ssm form is slightly more verbose; the aws-sm form requires the import. Workspace style elsewhere is mixed.

Recommendation: pick one and apply consistently. A small `use std::sync::Arc;` at the top of aws-ssm/src/lib.rs reduces line length in the inventory blocks and matches aws-sm.

Bikeshedding-adjacent — file at P4. Mirror of 8bh8.9 spirit (different mechanism: 8bh8.9 was redundant-fully-qualified because Arc was already imported; here Arc is NOT imported).
title: secretx-aws-ssm: inventory::submit! uses fully-qualified std::sync::Arc; sibling crate aws-sm imports it
